danger_signals.visual_form: Flame diminishes and dies in torch inventory/UI
danger_signals.meaning: Critical survival threat—player stumbles in darkness and breaks neck if all light sources lost

danger_signals.visual_form: Pitch-black rooms and passages with no visibility
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate hazard; player cannot navigate without lit torch

danger_signals.visual_form: Text descriptions of creatures (dragons, cyclops, wolf-woman, sharks)
Text warnings about unstable structures, acidic slime, weightlessness, heavy objects
danger_signals.meaning: Puzzle failure will result in specific death condition

danger_signals.visual_form: Skeleton hand cursor (international release) vs. armored glove (JP)
danger_signals.meaning: Reinforces dark tone and constant threat of death

danger_signals.visual_form: Description: 'earth seems to split in two as darkness begins to appear'
danger_signals.meaning: Final boss arena; point of no return

safety_signals.visual_form: Torches found as collectible items in inventory
safety_signals.meaning: Resource secured; player has extended survival window

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapons, gauntlets, artifacts appear in inventory
safety_signals.meaning: Tools available to solve upcoming puzzles or defeat enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Three artifacts combine into Staff of Ages
safety_signals.meaning: Path to final victory secured; capable of defeating Behemoth

safety_signals.visual_form: Fine stitchwork and intricate patterns on floor coverings
safety_signals.meaning: Benign/civilized areas; civilized world still exists beyond castle

safety_signals.visual_form: Descriptions mention 'well-lit tunnel' with natural/magical illumination
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage available; no torch consumption required

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Behemoth demon summoning; gates of Hell
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dragon, Cyclops, Wolf-woman, Sharks, Troll (toll collector)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle Shadowgate; cavern where earth splits; dark magic permeates structure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Staff of Ages (holy weapon); three sacred artifacts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wizard Lakmir's influence; King's daughter waiting at end; Westland realm

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Troll guard with spear; becomes 'angry and frustrated' when magic obscures vision
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Troll Bridge location; neutral/transactional relationship with player

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Castle unopened / Warlock Lord gathering power
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gates of Hell sealed; Behemoth not yet summoned
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate castle, collect artifacts, progress through increasingly dangerous zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Torches burning down (time limit visualization); inventory accumulating weapons and artifacts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies and traps become more elaborate; puzzles increase in complexity and lethality

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Three artifacts collected / Staff of Ages assembly possible
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three sacred artifacts visible in inventory; can be combined into unified weapon
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains combat capability; path to final confrontation opens

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earth splits; darkness appears from cavern; Behemoth emerges
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final combat initiated; player must use Staff to mortally wound demon

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Behemoth defeated / Warlock Lord dragged to Hell
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Victory achieved; player returned to civilized realm
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player betrothed to King's daughter; named High Lord of Westland; world saved

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pitch-black visuals; no environmental details visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player stumbles; neck breaks; forced to reload from save

environmental_storytelling.example: Limited torch supply forces strategic movement through castle; 'The flame from the torch is dying out' messages create urgency and dread
Castle Shadowgate designed as layered fortress; journey from entry to Warlock Lord's inner cavern mirrors ascent through enemy territory
Dragon's breath, shark lake, acidic slime, weightlessness chasm—each location tells story of Warlock Lord's dominion through specific trap types
Artifacts scattered throughout castle suggest previous heroes attempted same quest; 'crudely-made eight-foot club' and heavy shields indicate warrior tradition
References to dark ages, Elven craftsmanship, wizard Lakmir, Westland kingdom establish broader political/historical context
Different creature types (troll, dragon, cyclops, wolf-woman) suggest Warlock Lord commands diverse demonic/monstrous forces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Threatens with spear; demands bridge toll; remains hostile
Becomes 'angry and frustrated'; loses ability to threaten effectively; behavior becomes reactive rather than controlling
Magic overcome; spell effectiveness diminishes; player must find new solution
Breathes fire; insta-death if player unprotected
Attacks on sight; player must use appropriate weapon or die
Eat player who enters water; insta-death if undefended
Opens Hell gates; summons Behemoth; requires Staff of Ages to defeat; dragged to Hell upon Behemoth's death

notes: Shadowgate communicates world-state primarily through text-based descriptions and resource management (torches). The NES version uses cursor variants (skeleton hand vs. armored glove) as regional identity signals. Danger is communicated through: (1) torch depletion mechanics creating survival pressure, (2) graphic death descriptions warning of puzzle consequences, and (3) monster encounter descriptions. Political situation is minimal but present: player is 'last of a great line of hero-kings' serving wizard Lakmir against Warlock Lord's summoning plot; victory results in nobility (High Lord of Westland). The game lacks faction armor/banner visuals typical of console RPGs; instead uses location-based environmental storytelling (dark castle = evil domain; civilized artifacts = good resistance). Trial-and-error puzzle design means most wrong actions trigger instant death with sardonic text commentary, embedding danger into the core gameplay loop.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Shadowgate NES article)
